# Project Office — how to work in this repo

This repository is connected to a **project office** (a Backlog.md workspace at
`/home/igor/Documents/OBSIDIANT_SPACES/projects_offices/po_mvp_task_manager`) where its
tasks, drafts, milestones and documentation live, kept separate from this code repo.

<!-- project-office:managed:start -->
## Office rules (managed — regenerated by setup; edit "Working flow" below instead)

- **Everything office-side goes through the `backlog` CLI**, run from the office root:
  `(cd /home/igor/Documents/OBSIDIANT_SPACES/projects_offices/po_mvp_task_manager && backlog task list --plain / task view <id> --plain / doc list)`.
  Never hand-edit files under the office `src/`.
- **Read a doc body from its file** `/home/igor/Documents/OBSIDIANT_SPACES/projects_offices/po_mvp_task_manager/src/docs/<path>` (get `<path>` from
  `backlog doc list` / `doc search`). `backlog doc view` is an interactive TUI — never run it.
- **Status ownership:** the agent's terminal task status is `Ready To Test`; `Done`, `complete`
  and archive are **user-only**. There is no `Blocked` status.
- Tasks / drafts / milestones use the office `task` flow; office documentation uses the `docs`
  flow (only when the office is the explicit destination).
<!-- project-office:managed:end -->

## Working flow (defaults — edit or extend for this project)

Sensible starting conventions for how work moves through the office here. Change anything that
doesn't fit, and add your own rules under "Project-specific conventions".

### Tasks
- **Pick up a task** — move `To Do → In Progress` and assign yourself when you actually start
  work on it, not before.
- **While working** — append progress, decisions and validation as **notes**
  (`task edit <id> --append-notes "…"`); raise open questions or anything needing a decision as
  **comments** (`--comment "…"`). Don't overwrite the description with progress.
- **Acceptance criteria** — refine them only when the intended outcome genuinely changed; check
  each off (`--check-ac N`) as it is met.
- **Hand off** — move `In Progress → Ready To Test` **only after the user confirms** it's ready
  to test; add a short final summary of what changed and how it was verified.
- **Can't proceed** — don't park it in a status; leave it `In Progress` and raise it with the
  user.

### Docs
- Create or update office documentation only when explicitly asked to document into the office.
- Prefer updating an existing doc over creating a near-duplicate.

### Project-specific conventions
Add rules unique to this project here — for example: which repo is primary for API work, the
label taxonomy you use, or a requirement that a task carries a linked doc before it can move to
`Ready To Test`.
